old_claim.note: Electricity provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No direct dependency remains because the narrowed machine produces electrical current from mechanical rotation, while its precise generator lineage and conductor components are modeled separately.

source_shape.source_locator: Pages 367-369 define the machine as converting mechanical force into electrical force through an electromagnetic apparatus, with Faraday's generator work as the stated lineage.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The paper treats electricity as the machine's generated output and field of operation, not as a separate upstream machine or infrastructure component.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: The broad electricity node would duplicate the more precise Faraday-generator predecessor and does not identify what physically enters or enables the self-excited machine.

ontology_before: Dynamo inherited directly from the broad Electricity field while also claiming to be the first 1831 generator.
ontology_after: Self-Excited Industrial Dynamo inherits from the concrete Faraday generator lineage and insulated-winding capability; Electricity is no longer a generic direct edge.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: dynamo no longer directly depends on electricity.
would_reject_if: The electricity node is narrowed to a specific excitation supply component required by every self-excited dynamo.
A primary source identifies a separate electrical-power input as necessary for continuous operation of the scoped machine.
The Faraday generator predecessor edge is removed, leaving no representation of the earlier electrical-generation lineage.
short_reason: Electricity is the output domain, not the precise prerequisite.
